gaps.gap: Baseline is silent on how a null billable_rate should render in the project list — the correct behavior the defect exposes is under-specified.
interpretations: Render a null billable_rate as a blank placeholder (an em-dash '—') in the rate column; the row renders, no numeric format applied to a null value.
Render a null billable_rate as zero ('0.00') in the rate column, formatted like any numeric rate.
Omit the rate field entirely for a null-rate project; the row renders name and currency only.
gaps.recommended_default: Render a null billable_rate as a blank placeholder (an em-dash '—') in the rate column; the row renders, no numeric format applied to a null value.
gaps.reason: All three builds share the same data model, stack, and the same _render surface; the list-500 is settled-wrong actual behavior (the defect), not a fork. What moves is which spec-consistent display the correct behavior shows for the null-rate case — a presentation choice on the unchanged render path. No new capability and no new tech (bugfix delta Rule 3), so scope, not architecture.
